idea.hook: Hottest and coldest place... on the SAME planet!?
facts: During its long 59-Earth-day 'day,' Mercury's sun-facing side bakes at 430°C (800°F) – hot enough to melt lead!
With no atmosphere to hold heat, its night side plunges to -180°C (-290°F), colder than anywhere on Earth.
This incredible 610°C (1090°F) daily temperature swing is the most extreme of any planet in our Solar System.
idea.payoff: Mercury isn't just hot OR cold... it's BOTH to an unimaginable extreme!
idea.title: Mercury's EXTREME Temperature Swings! 🔥❄️

scenes.text: Hottest & Coldest Place... on the SAME Planet!?
scenes.visual: Starts with a wide shot of stars, then zooms in to reveal Mercury, clearly half in bright sunlight and half in deep shadow.
Close-up on Mercury's sun-facing side, glowing intensely orange/red, with subtle heat distortion effects. A small, stylized icon of melting lead appears briefly.
Transitions to a close-up of Mercury's night side, dark blue/purple with visible icy textures and frost effects. A small, stylized ice crystal icon appears briefly.

scenes.text: Daily Temp Swing: 610°C (1090°F) – Most extreme in Solar System!
scenes.visual: Mercury slowly rotates, showcasing the dramatic transition from the fiery day side to the frozen night side. A dynamic graphic of a thermometer indicating the extreme range appears then fades.

A full, impactful shot of Mercury, boldly split down the middle: one half a vibrant, fiery red-orange with intense heat waves, the other half an icy, deep blue with shimmering frost. The planet seems to radiate extreme energy.
